The let and const Hoisting Gotcha: Why It's ReferenceError, Not undefined

I ran into a JavaScript quiz that I was sure would log undefined & undefined — and it didn't. Turns out let and const do get hoisted, but they behave differently from var thanks to something called the Temporal Dead Zone (TDZ). This post records the question, the answer, and what's actually going on.

How Do You Write a Ternary Operator in CoffeeScript? (Spoiler: You Don't)

CoffeeScript doesn't support the usual JavaScript ternary operator syntax, so if you try the classic `a ? b : c` you'll just get an error. Here's a quick note on how to write the equivalent in CoffeeScript using if/then/else, including a couple of variations inside string interpolation.
